sections.title: 2022 AMENDMENTS
sections.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty-fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022, amendments were made in Section 7.5 Labelling of non-retail containers.
sections.retrieval_text:
2022 AMENDMENTS
level=1

page_spans.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty-fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022, amendments were made in Section 7.5 Labelling of non-retail containers.

sections.title: 2023 AMENDMENTS
sections.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty -sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December  2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.
sections.retrieval_text:
2023 AMENDMENTS
level=1
Following decisions taken at the Forty -sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December  2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.

page_spans.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty -sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December  2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to evaporated milks, intended for direct consumption or further processing, in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard applies to evaporated milks, intended for direct consumption or further processing, in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to evaporated milks, intended for direct consumption or further processing, in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Evaporated milks are milk products which can be obtained by the partial removal of water from milk by heat, or by any other process which leads to a product of the same composition and characteristics. The fat and/or protein content of the milk may have been adjusted, only to comply with the compositional requirements in Section 3 of this standard, by the addition and/or withdrawal of milk constituents in such a way as not to alter the whey protein to casein ratio of the milk being adjusted.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION
number=2
level=1
Evaporated milks are milk products which can be obtained by the partial removal of water from milk by heat, or by any other process which leads to a product of the same composition and characteristics. The fat and/or protein content of the milk may have been adjusted, only to comply with the compositional requirements in Section 3 of this standard, by the addition and/or withdrawal of milk constituents in such a way as not to alter the whey protein to casein ratio of the milk being adjusted.

page_spans.text: Evaporated milks are milk products which can be obtained by the partial removal of water from milk by heat, or by any other process which leads to a product of the same composition and characteristics. The fat and/or protein content of the milk may have been adjusted, only to comply with the compositional requirements in Section 3 of this standard, by the addition and/or withdrawal of milk constituents in such a way as not to alter the whey protein to casein ratio of the milk being adjusted.

sections.title: ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
number=3
level=1

sections.title: Raw materials
sections.text: Milk and milk powders,i cream and cream powders,ii milkfat products.iii The following milk products are allowed for protein adjustment purposes: – Milk retentate: Milk retentate is the product obtained by concentrating milk protein by ultrafiltration of milk, partly skimmed milk, or skimmed milk; – Milk permeate: Milk permeate is the product obtained by removing milk proteins and milkfat from milk, partly skimmed milk, or skimmed milk by ultrafiltration; and – Lactose.iv

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Only those additive functional classes indicated as technologically justified in the table below may be used for the product category specified. Acidity regulators used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 01.3.1 (Condensed milk [plain]) and only certain acidity regulators, emulsifiers, firming agents, stabilizers and thickeners, in Table 3 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard. Additive functional class Justified use in evaporated milks: Acidity regulators X Emulsifiers X Firming agents X Stabilizers X Thickeners X X The use of additives belonging to the class is technologically justified.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).2 The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)2 and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),3 the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004)4 and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).5

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)6 and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999),7 the following specific provisions apply:
sections.retrieval_text:

number=7
level=1

page_spans.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)6 and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999),7 the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name of the food shall be: Evaporated milk according to the composition specified in Section 3 Evaporated skimmed milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk Evaporated high-fat milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk may be designated “evaporated semi-skimmed milk” if the milkfat content is 4.0–4.5 percent and the minimum milk solids is 24 percent m/m.
sections.retrieval_text:
LABELLING > Name of the food
number=7.1
level=2
The name of the food shall be: Evaporated milk according to the composition specified in Section 3 Evaporated skimmed milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk Evaporated high-fat milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk may be designated “evaporated semi-skimmed milk” if the milkfat content is 4.0–4.5 percent and the minimum milk solids is 24 percent m/m.

page_spans.text: The name of the food shall be: Evaporated milk according to the composition specified in Section 3 Evaporated skimmed milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk Evaporated high-fat milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk may be designated “evaporated semi-skimmed milk” if the milkfat content is 4.0–4.5 percent and the minimum milk solids is 24 percent m/m.

sections.title: Declaration of milkfat content
sections.text: If the consumer would be misled by the omission, the milkfat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass or volume, or (ii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.

sections.title: List of ingredients
sections.text: Notwithstanding the provision of Section 4.2.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), milk products used only for protein adjustment need not be declared.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

LABELLING OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS

level=1

page_spans.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: METHODS OF SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.
sections.retrieval_text:
METHODS OF SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS
number=8
level=1
For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

page_spans.text: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

sentences.original: Only those additive functional classes indicated as technologically justified in the table below may be used for the product category specified.

sentences.original: Acidity regulators used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 01.3.1 (Condensed milk [plain]) and only certain acidity regulators, emulsifiers, firming agents, stabilizers and thickeners, in Table 3 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.

sentences.original: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).2 The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)2 and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).2 The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)2 and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
classification.function: Mandate compliance with maximum contaminant and residue limits
classification.pattern: Two linked obligation sentences specifying compliance requirements with standards

components.subject: products covered by this standard
components.requirement: comply with maximum levels for contaminants per General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed
components.additional requirement: milk used must comply with maximum levels and residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides

classification.justification: Use of 'shall comply' signals obligation for product and milk safety compliance.
notes: Strong normative obligation on limits compliance

sentences.original: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969),3 the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004)4 and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).5
classification.function: Express expected compliance with microbiological criteria
classification.pattern: Modal obligation recommending compliance with criteria established per guidelines

components.subject: The products
components.criteria source: Principles and Guidelines for Establishment of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: Modal 'should comply' expresses a normative expectation, a mild obligation.
notes: Normative recommendation close to obligation

sentences.original: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)6 and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999),7 the following specific provisions apply:

prefilter.reason: References specific standards and provisions.

classification.sentence: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)6 and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999),7 the following specific provisions apply:
classification.function: Introduce additional specific provisions beyond existing standards
classification.pattern: Introductory complex sentence referencing other standards to introduce provisions

additional_provisions_applied_besides: General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods
General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms

classification.justification: Serves as a preamble to specific provisions, no normative modality in itself.
notes: Introductory preamble

sentences.original: The name of the food shall be: Evaporated milk according to the composition specified in Section 3 Evaporated skimmed milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk Evaporated high-fat milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk may be designated “evaporated semi-skimmed milk” if the milkfat content is 4.0–4.5 percent and the minimum milk solids is 24 percent m/m.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: The name of the food shall be: Evaporated milk according to the composition specified in Section 3 Evaporated skimmed milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk Evaporated high-fat milk Evaporated partly skimmed milk may be designated “evaporated semi-skimmed milk” if the milkfat content is 4.0–4.5 percent and the minimum milk solids is 24 percent m/m.
classification.function: Mandate naming convention with conditional permission for designation
classification.pattern: Compound sentence expressing obligation followed by conditional permission

components.obligation: The name of the food shall be among specified options
components.permission_condition: Evaporated partly skimmed milk may be designated “evaporated semi-skimmed milk” if milkfat is 4.0–4.5% and minimum solids 24% m/m

classification.justification: Use of 'shall' shows obligation for naming; 'may be designated' allows conditional permission.
notes: Combined obligation and permission within naming rules

sentences.original: If the consumer would be misled by the omission, the milkfat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass or volume, or (ii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: If the consumer would be misled by the omission, the milkfat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass or volume, or (ii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.
classification.function: Impose declaration obligation conditioned on potential consumer misleading
classification.pattern: Conditional clause followed by mandatory action with specification options

components.condition: If the consumer would be misled by omission
components.obligation: milkfat content shall be declared in acceptable manner
declaration format options: grams per serving if servings stated

classification.justification: 'If' marks condition; 'shall be declared' marks obligation under that condition.
notes: Conditional obligation for labeling

sentences.original: If the consumer would be misled by the omission, the milk protein content shall be declared in a manner acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either as (i) a percentage by mass or volume, or (ii) grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: If the consumer would be misled by the omission, the milk protein content shall be declared in a manner acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either as (i) a percentage by mass or volume, or (ii) grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.
classification.function: Impose declaration obligation on milk protein content conditioned on consumer misleading
classification.pattern: Conditional clause followed by obligation with declaration format options

components.condition: If the consumer would be misled by omission
components.obligation: milk protein content shall be declared appropriately
formats: grams per serving if servings stated

classification.justification: Parallel structure to previous sentence with condition and obligation.
notes: Conditional labelling obligation

sentences.original: Notwithstanding the provision of Section 4.2.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), milk products used only for protein adjustment need not be declared.

prefilter.reason: Specific regulatory exemption regarding labeling.

classification.sentence: Notwithstanding the provision of Section 4.2.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), milk products used only for protein adjustment need not be declared.
classification.function: Allow omission of declaration for milk products used exclusively for protein adjustment
classification.pattern: Exception statement granting permission to omit declaration despite general standard

components.exception: Notwithstanding Section 4.2.1 of General Standard for Labelling
components.permission: milk products used only for protein adjustment need not be declared

classification.justification: 'Need not be declared' implies permission to omit declaration as exception.
notes: Exception to general labelling obligation

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase indicating purpose + main clause prescribing required methods and reference standards with modal verb 'shall'

components.purpose: For checking the compliance with this standard
components.required_items: methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard

classification.justification: The sentence prescribes an obligation to use specified methods for compliance checking with normative modality 'shall'. The sentence structure includes an introductory purpose phrase followed by a prescriptive main clause. No known templates match closely, so a new pattern reflecting this structure is created.
notes: The sentence combines normative obligation with purpose indication in a complex sentence; no direct template found in catalog.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR EVAPORATED MILKS
pages.summary: A página contém o título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR EVAPORATED MILKS' e o cabeçalho 'CXS 281-1971', mas não possui seções, subtítulos ou texto explicativo para mapear. Nenhuma hierarquia estrutural identificada nesta página.

Página contém dois títulos principais em caixa alta sem numeração: '2022 AMENDMENTS' e '2023 AMENDMENTS', cada um com seu texto explicativo respectivo. Nenhuma continuidade com páginas anteriores ou próximas foi detectada.

A página contém três seções principais numeradas: '1. SCOPE', '2. DESCRIPTION', e '3. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS'. A seção 3 é subdividida em três subseções numeradas: '3.1 Raw materials', '3.2 Permitted ingredients' e '3.3 Composition'. O texto está associado corretamente a cada título, com títulos exibidos visivelmente na página e sem indícios de continuidade para outras páginas.
Página com 4 seções principais numeradas (4 a 7). Seção 7 possui uma subseção (7.1). Texto associado corretamente a cada título. Títulos exibidos na página e sem continuidade para outras páginas.

Página com seções numeradas 7.2 a 8, todas exibindo título visualmente e com texto associado. Também possui seção final NOTES com referências, com texto completo na página. Todas as seções indicam continuidade desde página anterior, sem continuação para próxima página.
